text.title: Technical Debt Trade-off Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns commonly observed in debt-backlogs. Each pairs symptom + root-cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Debt-record's repayment_trigger reads "someday" / "when we have time" / "Q2-ish". The item never closes; backlog grows for 18 months.
antipattern.root-cause: Author skipped observable-trigger work because the shortcut already shipped.
antipattern.fix: Reject any trigger missing {metric, operator, threshold, source}; require numeric or enum threshold. See rule observable-trigger-required.

antipattern.symptom: Team logs "switch database in 2 years" as a DEBT-XXXX item. Two years later nobody owns the migration; it becomes a P0 outage.
antipattern.root-cause: Author confused a systemic architectural flaw with a localized shortcut.
antipattern.fix: When code_area touches ≥3 modules or names a foundation choice, force escalation to ATAM; refuse to issue a DEBT id. See rule localized-or-systemic.

antipattern.symptom: Backlog adds every shortcut as debt; total debt cost reaches 35% of capacity; feature velocity halves; team blames "engineering".
antipattern.root-cause: No gate prevents new debt when total > budget.
antipattern.fix: Block new debt when current_total_debt_pct + budget_cost_pct > 20; force repayment first. See rule debt-budget-cap.

antipattern.symptom: Code review approves a workaround; no debt-record created. Six months later the workaround is "how it works" and the better solution is forgotten.
antipattern.fix: Pre-commit hook flags TODO/FIXME/HACK comments without a linked DEBT-XXXX id; CI breaks on unlinked workarounds. See rule fowler-quadrant-required.
